user_prompt: read `lib\FlyGui\readme.md` and implement the design, make sure to comment where something is implemented according to a part of my design
agent_reply: - fast vs slow poll mode, with slow mode capped at 15 Hz
- top bar drawing for RTC date/time and battery
- sibling image-buffer lookup to avoid duplicate RAM loads
- partial redraw of changed character ranges
I also added the requested per-screen files under `lib/FlyGuiViews`, one class/file pair per view, starting at [MainScreenView.h](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/MainScreenView.h:5).
I added comments prefixed with `Design:` at the places where code directly implements a part of your readme design.
- A targeted `pio ci` compile did compile the new FlyGui sources cleanly; its final link failed only because the temporary CI input was missing unrelated app demo sources called by `main.cpp`.

user_prompt: read `lib\ClockAgent\readme.md` and implement inside `lib\ClockAgent`, then update any existing usages of the RTC to use the ClockAgent
agent_reply: - Provides a global `Clock` object with an RTC-like API:
- Reads cache the RTC timestamp and advance it using `millis()`, avoiding repeated I2C RTC reads.
- Writes still call through to `M5.Rtc` and update the cached clock immediately.
Updated existing RTC reads to use `Clock` in:
`M5.Rtc` is now only used inside `ClockAgent`, where it belongs.
- Provides a global `Clock` object with an RTC-like API:
- Reads cache the RTC timestamp and advance it using `millis()`, avoiding repeated I2C RTC reads.
- Writes still call through to `M5.Rtc` and update the cached clock immediately.
Updated existing RTC reads to use `Clock` in:
`M5.Rtc` is now only used inside `ClockAgent`, where it belongs.

user_prompt: In `ErrorView`, move the sprite and the message text down by 20 pixels, as it is conflicting with the top bar
also you need to signal to the top bar to do a full refresh instead of a partial text refresh, because you used `display.fillScreen(TFT_BLACK);`.
also, you have written some text drawing helper functions that might be reused, move those into `lib\FlyGui\FlyGuiText.cpp` and `lib\FlyGui\FlyGuiText.h`
agent_reply: - Moved the ErrorView icon down from `y=4` to `y=24`.
- Moved the wrapped error message down from `y=120` to `y=140`.
- `ErrorView` now requests a full top-bar redraw after `display.fillScreen(TFT_BLACK)`.
- `FlyGui::redraw()` now immediately redraws the top bar again if a view invalidates it mid-redraw.

- Moved the word-wrap helper into `FlyGuiTextUtil::drawWrappedText(...)` in [FlyGuiText.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGuiText.h) and [FlyGuiText.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGuiText.cpp).
- Moved `sprite_desc_t` into [IconLookup.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/SpriteDraw/IconLookup.h), since `inc/sprites.h` is generated and got overwritten during build.

user_prompt: `drawTopBar` needs to stop drawing the battery status as percent, and instead, use the new sprites that look like `sprit_batt_*`
also, remove all mentions of `imagePath` from `FlyGuiItem`, and get rid of `drawItemSprite`. Everything uses sprites now but not all icons will have a string
agent_reply: Done. `drawTopBar()` now uses the `sprit_batt_*` PNG sprites instead of drawing battery percent text, including charging variants.
I also removed the `imagePath` concept from `FlyGuiItem`:
- `FlyGuiItem` now only handles text/touch/base item behavior.

One related cleanup: `sprite_desc_t` now lives in [IconLookup.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/SpriteDraw/IconLookup.h), since [sprites.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/sprites.h) is generated and should not own that API type.

commit_message: good progress working on GUI but ran into memory problems with PNG decode

user_prompt: avoid having `M5GFX& display` as a parameter for functions and methods, there should be a global reference to the display for anything unrelated to FlyGui, and if FlyGui is involved, there should be a way to dig through trees to get a reference to it, or just use the global reference
agent_reply: - Updated direct display users to use `thefly_display` instead of threading a local `display` reference or calling `M5.Display` directly.
- Kept `FlyGui::display()` as a convenience getter that returns the global display, so tree-based GUI code can still ask the GUI for its screen if needed.

user_prompt: find me places where we are not using 1.0f
agent_reply: Places currently using non-`1.0f` text scaling:
`kTextSize = 2.0f`, used for duration and caller info.

Worth noting: [FlyGuiText.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGuiText.cpp:201) calls `setTextSize(fontSize_)`, so any `FlyGuiText` instance can be scaled indirectly. The top bar date/time uses `1.0f`; the RecordingView text instances are the non-`1.0f` ones I found.

user_prompt: the rendering of the text for caller info, it's not using a fixed width font, so the rendering leaves artifacts that we assumed would've been overwritten
that chunk of text should either use a fixed width font, or be totally cleared on update by a black rectangle
agent_reply: Fixed. I added `FlyGuiText::setClearOnUpdate(true)` and enabled it only for `callerInfoText_` in `RecordingView`.
So that caller-info line now clears its full black rectangle before drawing the next rotating value, while the rest of the text system can keep its lighter partial redraw behavior.
Fixed. I added `FlyGuiText::setClearOnUpdate(true)` and enabled it only for `callerInfoText_` in `RecordingView`.
So that caller-info line now clears its full black rectangle before drawing the next rotating value, while the rest of the text system can keep its lighter partial redraw behavior.

user_prompt: edit the top bar clock a bit, if the command line parameters says that debug is enabled (like the ESP_LOG level is higher than error), then instead of the year like 2026, make it say DEBUG
agent_reply: Done. In [FlyGuiText.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGuiText.cpp), the top bar clock now shows:
when either `LOG_LOCAL_LEVEL` or `CORE_DEBUG_LEVEL` is higher than `ESP_LOG_ERROR`. Normal/error-only builds still show:
I also bumped the clock text buffer length by one so `DEBUG` does not truncate the timestamp.
